# criblio_search_dataset_ruleset (Resource)

SearchDatasetRuleset Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_search_dataset_ruleset" "my_search_dataset_ruleset" {
  id = "default"
  rules = [
    {
      dataset = "secondary_ds"
      id = "secondary"
      kusto_expression = "sourcetype == \"test\""
      name = "secondary route"
    }
    {
      dataset = "main"
      description = "data catch all"
      id = "default"
      kusto_expression = "*"
      name = "main"
      send_data_to = "destinationDataset"
    }
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `rules` (Attributes List) Ordered rules for routing events to Datasets. The first matching rule wins. Each rule can include an extend expression to transform matched events. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules))

### Optional

- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the ruleset.

<a id="nestedatt--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Required:

- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the rule.
- `kusto_expression` (String) Kusto predicate that selects events for this rule.
- `name` (String) Display name of the rule.

Optional:

- `dataset` (String) The <code>id</code> of the target Dataset to route matched events to. Required when <code>sendDataTo</code> is <code>destinationDataset</code>. Ignored when <code>sendDataTo</code> is <code>drop</code>.
- `description` (String) Brief description of the rule.
- `disabled` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, the rule is ignored for routing.
- `extend_expression` (String) KQL-style extend expression to add or transform fields on matched events.
- `extend_expression_enabled` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, the <code>extendExpression</code> is applied for the rule.
- `send_data_to` (String) To send matching events to a Dataset, <code>destinationDataset</code>. To drop matching events, <code>drop</code>.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_search_dataset_ruleset.my_criblio_search_dataset_ruleset
  id = "default"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_search_dataset_ruleset.my_criblio_search_dataset_ruleset "default"
```
